majors.count_method: official UChicago Areas of Study page says the College includes nearly 70 majors and 60 minors
titles: History, Philosophy, and Social Studies of Science and Medicine

majors.notes: Official UChicago Areas of Study page says the College includes nearly 70 majors and 60 minors; titles below were extracted from entries marked M (major) on that page.

admissions.application_platform: UChicago accepts Apply Coalition, Powered by Scoir or the Common Application.
admissions.testing_policy: UChicago is test-optional. It says SAT or ACT scores are optional and not required for admission, uses a No Harm testing policy, and allows self-reported scores with official reports required only after admission and enrollment.
admissions.gpa_policy: UChicago does not publish a minimum GPA. Instead, it reads the transcript across all four years to see whether students challenged themselves productively and performed well in the courses available at their school.
admissions.course_rigor: UChicago does not publish a fixed course checklist, but it says the transcript should show that students challenged themselves productively across four years and took advantage of the opportunities available at their high school.
admissions.recommendations: UChicago requires a secondary school report and transcript plus two teacher recommendations from academic-subject teachers who know the applicant’s academic interests and classroom contributions.
admissions.essays: UChicago requires the main application personal statement plus two UChicago supplement essays: one extended essay from its prompt list and one short why-UChicago essay.

projects_research: UChicago says extracurriculars can include anything meaningful outside class and home-schooled applicants often submit research abstracts among their supplemental materials.

evidence.source_excerpt: All of the University of Chicago's nearly 70 majors and 60 minors, as well as dozens of areas of specialized study and pre-professional preparation, are part of one undergraduate College.

evidence.claim: UChicago is test-optional, applies a No Harm review policy, and allows self-reported scores with official reports required only for enrolling admitted students.
evidence.source_excerpt: Submitting an SAT or ACT is optional and not required for admission ... UChicago practices a "No Harm" policy ... Students sharing self-reported scores will not be required to submit official score reports unless they are admitted and choose to enroll.

evidence.claim: UChicago requires a secondary school report and transcript plus two teacher recommendations from academic-subject teachers.
evidence.source_excerpt: Ask your secondary school counselor to complete the Secondary School Report ... We require two recommendations from teachers who have taught you in an academic subject ...

evidence.claim: UChicago requires a personal statement and two supplemental essays: one extended essay and one short why-UChicago essay.
evidence.source_excerpt: If you apply to the University of Chicago, you will also submit two supplemental essays ... one extended essay ... and one short essay on why you would like to attend the University of Chicago.

evidence.claim: UChicago’s application guidance treats meaningful extracurricular work broadly, and for home-schooled applicants it explicitly lists research abstracts as an example supplemental material.
evidence.source_excerpt: Supplemental Materials ... Extracurricular projects such as artwork, a research abstract, or writing.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 20 professors from RateMyProfessors by rating count and sentiment metrics.
evidence.source_excerpt: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'University of Chicago' (legacyId=1085) and ranked up to 20 professors with at least one rating.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 20 notable alumni from Wikipedia-derived candidate lists using trailing pageviews.
evidence.source_excerpt: Candidates were harvested from school-specific Wikipedia alumni/people pages or categories, filtered to likely people, and ranked by pageviews. Major-specific and last-10-years filtering requires stronger school-specific alumni sources than this generic pipeline currently has.

evidence.claim: Extracted 60 official undergraduate major titles from the official UChicago areas-of-study listing.
evidence.source_excerpt: Anthropology; Archaeology; Art History; Astrophysics; Biological Chemistry; Biological Sciences; Chemistry; Cinema and Media Studies; Classical Studies; Climate and Sustainable Growth; Cognitive Science; Comparative Human Development

evidence.claim: UChicago accepts Apply Coalition, Powered by Scoir or the Common Application.
evidence.source_excerpt: UChicago accepts Apply Coalition, Powered by Scoir or the Common Application.

evidence.claim: UChicago does not publish a minimum GPA and instead reviews the transcript across all four years in the context of the school.
evidence.source_excerpt: In reading your application, your transcript will serve as a roadmap of your academic path in high school. We will be looking at your academic record across all four years of high school ... Your transcript is considered in the context of your high school.

evidence.claim: UChicago says it looks for students who challenged themselves productively in available coursework rather than publishing a fixed minimum course formula.
evidence.source_excerpt: We will be looking ... primarily to see that you have challenged yourself productively in your course selection and done well in those courses. Course offerings and opportunities can look very different from one high school to the next, and we want to see how you took advantage of what was available to you.

warnings: Major titles extracted from the official UChicago Areas of Study list by keeping entries marked M for majors.
Phase 4 structured upgrade applied to top-10 expansion schools.
School-people enrichment uses public third-party sources (RateMyProfessors and Wikipedia/Wikimedia), so rankings are heuristic rather than official university data.

popular_professors.ranking_basis: RateMyProfessors entries sorted by rating_count desc, then average_rating desc, then would_take_again_percent desc.
popular_professors.source_summary: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'University of Chicago' (legacyId=1085) and ranked up to 20 professors with at least one rating.

successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Curated from official UChicago Magazine and University of Chicago News person-specific alumni features, prioritizing pages with explicit class-year evidence and substantive descriptions of post-UChicago impact.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Extracted from official UChicago Magazine and University of Chicago News pages on mag.uchicago.edu and news.uchicago.edu.

items.name: Satya Nadella
items.bio: University of Chicago News says Satya Nadella, MBA’97, was promoted to CEO of Microsoft after 22 years at the company and after leading its Cloud and Enterprise group.
items.confirmation_label: Official University of Chicago News story
items.evidence_quality_note: Official university news story with explicit alumni degree-year evidence and substantive career summary.

items.name: Roger Ebert
items.bio: University of Chicago News says Roger Ebert, X’70, became one of the world’s most influential film critics and served for 37 years as a Graham School film lecturer.
items.description: Pulitzer-winning film critic and longtime UChicago lecturer.
items.confirmation_label: Official University of Chicago News story
items.evidence_quality_note: Official university news story with explicit UChicago alumni notation and substantive career summary; accessible page metadata surfaces X’70 rather than a completed degree year.

items.name: John Paul Stevens
items.bio: University of Chicago News says John Paul Stevens, AB’41, served on the U.S. Supreme Court for 34 years after building a distinguished legal career in Chicago.
items.confirmation_label: Official University of Chicago News story
items.evidence_quality_note: Official university news story with explicit alumni degree-year evidence and substantive career summary.
